items.title: Tracking Baillie Gifford's 13F Portfolio - Q4 2025 Update
Bills Are Paid Late, 'A Week Later They're Due Again,' Says Caller Earning $75K — Dave Ramsey Snaps, 'I Don't Give A Crap, Go Eat Leftovers'
items.description: Paying the bills never seems to move the needle for one couple, no matter how carefully they budget. Janet, from Boise, Idaho, called "The Ramsey Show," saying that despite trying to plan ahead, she and her husband feel trapped in a cycle where bills come due again almost immediately after they are paid. "It seems like every single month we pay our bills three weeks after the due date, and then a week later they're due again," she told hosts Dave Ramsey and Ken Coleman. The couple earns about $7

items.title: Barclays Sees 2026 as a Pivotal Year for Comcast Corporation (CMCSA) and the Broader Telecom Landscape
items.description: Comcast Corporation (NASDAQ:CMCSA) is included among Dividend Contenders List: Top 20 Stocks. On January 13, Barclays analyst Kannan Venkateshwar lowered the firm’s price target on Comcast Corporation (NASDAQ:CMCSA) to $28 from $30 and maintained an Equal Weight rating. The change came as part of a broader adjustment to price targets across the cable, satellite, and […]

items.title: 'We Would Likely See A Significant Contraction,' SoFi CEO Anthony Noto Says Of Trump's 10% Cap Plan — Consumers 'Will Still Need Access To Credit'
items.description: A policy meant to make borrowing cheaper could end up changing who gets access to credit at all. That is the likely outcome of a proposed 10% cap on credit card interest rates, according to SoFi Technologies (NASDAQ:SOFI) CEO Anthony Noto. In a recent post on X, Noto responded to President Donald Trump's proposal, saying the immediate effect would likely be a contraction in credit card lending rather than a drop in consumer demand for borrowing.
